fields.Name: MemberSuite
fields.Description: MemberSuite AMS connector — syncs members, organizations, memberships, chapters, events, registrations, orders, and certifications via MemberSuite REST API v2.

Auth.note: MemberSuite REST API v2 uses a two-step auth flow: (1) POST /platform/v2/msc_authdata/{tenantId} with a signed credential set (6-part: accessKeyId + associationId + associationKey + secretAccessKey + signingCertificate + signingCertificateId) to obtain an AuthenticationData {accessToken, idToken, refreshToken}; (2) Pass the accessToken as the Authorization header value on all subsequent data requests. The swagger also documents an alternate Cognito-based route via /platform/v2/authdata/{tenantId} with username+password+clientID+userPoolID, but the primary machine-to-machine route per the operator scope decision is the 6-part signed-request model.
Auth.headerNote: The swagger documents the Authorization header parameter as type string with description "access token" — no "Bearer " prefix is documented in the OpenAPI spec. The raw accessToken value is passed directly.

Configuration.AuthFlowNote: two-step: POST /platform/v2/msc_authdata/{tenantId} with 6-part signed credentials → receive accessToken → use as Authorization header on all data endpoints. The /platform/v2/refreshtoken endpoint is documented for token refresh.
Configuration.HostTemplateNote: Base URL is a shared, non-tenant-specific host (https://rest.membersuite.com). Tenancy is resolved via the tenantId path parameter on list endpoints (GET /{service}/v1/{resource}/{tenantId}) and via the auth token context. No per-account hostname variation. Source: swagger host field "rest.membersuite.com" (all 8 specs).

PaginationDefaults.pageParamNote: 1-indexed (first page = 1, not 0). Source: SOURCE_STUDY §API Conventions point 2.
PaginationDefaults.filterSyntax: MSQL (MemberSuite Query Language) — SQL-like filter syntax specific to this vendor. Example: msql=select name from certification where active = true

Configuration.PaginationNote: All list endpoints use offset-based page/pageSize pagination. No cursor mechanism. page parameter is 1-indexed. No documented max pageSize in the swagger specs or Postman collection.
Configuration.APIVersioningNote: Service version is embedded in the URL path: /{service}/v1/{resource} (e.g., /crm/v1/individuals, /events/v1/events). Platform service is v2. Services are versioned independently (CRM=v1, Membership=v1, Events=v1, Orders=v1, Certifications=v1, Fundraising=v1, Platform=v2).
Configuration.TokenRefreshNote: Platform swagger documents GET /platform/v2/refreshtoken?refreshToken=<token> and POST /platform/v2/refreshtoken with RefreshTokenRequest {idToken, refreshToken}. Refresh token is returned in the initial AuthenticationData response alongside the accessToken.
Configuration.IncrementalSyncNote: All in-scope objects across all 6 services expose a lastModifiedDate field in their swagger definitions (format: date-time). Incremental sync uses MSQL filter on lastModifiedDate: msql=select * from {objectType} where lastModifiedDate > {watermark}. The watermark field is lastModifiedDate uniformly across all services.
Configuration.WebhooksNote: No webhook/event-subscription endpoint is documented in any of the 8 swagger specs or the Postman collection. No push notification mechanism is documented in the public API surface. Incremental sync must use MSQL date-filter polling.
Configuration.BulkOperationsNote: No bulk-ingest or batch-write endpoint is documented in the swagger specs. Individual-record CRUD only (POST/PUT/DELETE per record). Some objects have special bulk operations (e.g., /memberships/bulkDelete) but these are object-specific delete operations, not a general batch API.

ErrorResponseShape.description: MemberSuite REST API v2 returns standard HTTP status codes. The CRM swagger defines a ConciergeError schema with code (integer enum 0-35), message (string), offendingField (string), and id (string). Auth errors return HTTP 401 Unauthorized with body "Authorization denied. Check Authorization token and Security settings for this user".

offendingField.description: Field name that caused the validation error, when applicable

ErrorResponseShape.authErrorBody: Authorization denied. Check Authorization token and Security settings for this user
ErrorResponseShape.notFoundExample: Could not find the specified record in order to delete it

WriteCapability.note: All 6 in-scope services document CRUD operations (POST/PUT/DELETE) for most resources. The DELETE path uses a non-standard infix: DELETE /{service}/v1/{resource}/delete/{id} — NOT the REST standard DELETE /{resource}/{id}. Async operations return a job ID; poll /v1/asyncstatus/{id} for completion. Financial writes (order processing /orders/process) require additional business logic and are narrower in scope.

Configuration.DeleteSemanticsNote: The swagger documents DELETE /{service}/v1/{resource}/delete/{id} as a hard delete. No soft-delete archive flag or tombstone mechanism is documented in the public swagger specs. Records have an isActive field (boolean) on some object types, but no explicit "archive via isActive=false" documented as the standard delete pattern.
Configuration.ConcurrencyControlNote: No ETag, If-Match, version, or If-Unmodified-Since header is documented in any of the 8 swagger specs or the Postman collection. PUT operations take the full record body with no conflict-detection mechanism documented.
Configuration.DiscoveryIsAuthoritativeRationale: DiscoveryIsAuthoritative is false for three reasons: (1) the credential-free Declared metadata is seeded from the 6 in-scope swagger specs, not a single enumerate-all endpoint; (2) custom-field discovery via /platform/v2/customfields/{tenantId} and saved-search discovery via /platform/v2/savedsearches/{tenantId} are partial/scoped — each tenant has different custom fields and saved searches, so absence in one tenant's discovery proves nothing about the canonical object set; (3) the /description/{tenantid} field-schema endpoint is auth-gated and per-object, not a single authoritative catalog endpoint. Absence in a discovery refresh must never trigger deactivation of Declared metadata.

universalPK.note: All swagger definitions across all 6 in-scope services consistently expose an id field (type string) as the record identifier. Evidence: 100% of resource definitions across CRM, Membership, Events, Orders, Certifications, Fundraising swagger files include the id field. Get-by-ID paths use /{resource}/{id} pattern. This is the vendor-wide PK convention.
universalPK.coverage: 172/172 resource definitions with an id field across all 6 in-scope swagger services

Configuration.idempotencyNote: Activity and certification writebacks (the primary write-path) use an idempotency field to prevent duplicate writes. The field name is customer-configured (typically external_activity_id or a vendor-defined external reference field). Source: MemberSuiteContext customer config template Writeback section.
Configuration.DefaultSyncDirectionNote: Read-only (Pull) is the default for all objects. The API supports full CRUD for most objects, but write operations against a live association management system carry significant risk (member record corruption, membership status changes). Bidirectional must be validated against a sandbox before enabling. Activity and certification writebacks are the only approved write-back use cases per the MemberSuiteContext.
Configuration.DefaultConflictResolutionNote: Recommended DestWins (MemberSuite wins) because: (1) MemberSuite is the system of record for member data; (2) ConcurrencyControl is none — no ETag/version for optimistic locking, so MostRecent cannot be used safely; (3) In an AMS context, the AMS is authoritative for member status, membership expiration, and certification records.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: Legacy XML/WSDL SOAP envelope interface. Last documented update 2016. Superseded by REST API v2. Not JSON/REST — requires a separate SOAP connector pattern. The Concierge API docs at api.docs.membersuite.com cover this route.

Alternate route to the same member data via /graphql/v2. Introspection is auth-gated (returns 401), no public SDL published. The Postman collection shows 15 sample GraphQL queries (GetIndividual, GetOrganization, GetMembershipTypes, GetChapters, GetEvent, GetInvoices, etc.) but this is not the operator-scoped connector route. Revisit if GraphQL proves materially more complete than REST v2 for a specific object family.

Alternate Open API / Concierge route that pre-dates the REST v2 API. The Concierge API doc at api.docs.membersuite.com (AWS S3 hosted, last modified 2016-03-09) describes the original MemberSuite Open API. Out of scope in favor of the current REST v2 JSON surface.

Saved searches (/platform/v2/savedsearches, /platform/v2/scheduledsearches) are a runtime query mechanism — each tenant defines their own saved searches. These are reached as a RUNTIME discovery mechanism (auth-gated) and are per-tenant, not a standard object family. The Higher Logic marketing route uses saved searches as its primary sync mechanism. Out of scope for the standard object connector but documented for runtime discovery.

SSO / Portal 5 identity routing. These are identity/access management surfaces (/platform/v2/bearerTokenSSO, portal login URL, Portal 5 URL) — not data-sync objects. SSO is customer-configured and requires per-customer Portal 5 URL and tenant-specific configuration. Out of scope for the data connector.

Higher Logic community and marketing/Real Magnet routes are partner middleware products with separate APIs. MemberSuite provides the member data; Higher Logic provides the community/email platform. Integration between them is via the Higher Logic-MemberSuite Technical Worksheet (saved-search + Periodic Refresh / Member Refresh model). These are partner routes, not native MemberSuite API routes, and are out of scope.

LMS integrations (TopClass, Forj Journey by Forj, Slayte) are partner middleware that uses MemberSuite SSO for authentication and activity/certification writebacks for completion sync. The LMS partner route is implemented at the partner level — the MemberSuite connector covers the activity writeback (POST /crm/v1/activities) and certification writeback (POST /certifications/v1/certifications) endpoints that LMS routes call. The partner-side LMS routing is out of scope.

Event/submission partner routes (Slayte, abstract submission systems, awards platforms) use MemberSuite as a member directory for co-author lookup and speaker profiles, plus SSO for authentication. These are partner integrations that read from MemberSuite — covered by the standard CRM/Events read objects. The partner routing itself is out of scope.

Platform (v2) and Security (v1) REST API services are infrastructure/identity management, not syncable member data. Platform covers auth tokens, SSO, custom-object schema definition, association configuration. Security covers roles, policies, portal users, access credentials. These are classified INFORMATIONAL and are not in scope for data sync. 47 objects total (Platform: 30, Security: 17).
